content.problemStatement:
JJ has an array AAA of length NNN and an integer XXX. JJ can perform the following operation **at most once**:


Select a subsequence of AAA and add XXX to all the elements of that subsequence.
For example, if A=[2,1,6,3,5]A = [2, 1, 6, 3, 5]A=[2,1,6,3,5] and X=7X = 7X=7, we can select the subsequence [2,3,5][2, 3, 5][2,3,5] and add XXX to all the elements. Now the array AAA becomes [2+7,1,6,3+7,5+7]=[9,1,6,10,12][2 + 7, 1, 6, 3 + 7, 5 + 7] = [9, 1, 6, 10, 12][2+7,1,6,3+7,5+7]=[9,1,6,10,12].

JJ wants to maximize the value of ÔłĹi=2N(AiÔłĺ1ÔŐĽAi)\displaystyle \sum_{i = 2}^{N} (A_{i - 1} \oplus A_{i})i=2ÔłĹNÔÇő(AiÔłĺ1ÔÇőÔŐĽAiÔÇő). Can you help him to do so?

Here, ÔŐĽ\oplusÔŐĽ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

& & kya
&


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ÔÇö the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX ÔÇö the size of the array AAA and the parameter XXX mentioned in the statement.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,ÔÇŽ,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1ÔÇő,A2ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ANÔÇő denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum value of ÔłĹi=2n(AiÔłĺ1ÔŐĽAi)\displaystyle \sum_{i = 2}^{n} (A_{i - 1} \oplus A_{i})i=2ÔłĹnÔÇő(AiÔłĺ1ÔÇőÔŐĽAiÔÇő) which can be obtained after applying the given operation **at most once**.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2Ôőů1052 \cdot 10^52Ôőů105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It is optimal to not perform the given operation. So the answer will equal 1ÔŐĽ2=31 \oplus 2 = 31ÔŐĽ2=3.
Test case 222: It is optimal to add X=1X = 1X=1 to the 2nd2^{nd}2nd and the 3rd3^{rd}3rd element. So AAA will become [2,3,4,3][2, 3, 4, 3][2,3,4,3] and the answer will be (2ÔŐĽ3)+(3ÔŐĽ4)+(4ÔŐĽ3)=15(2 \oplus 3) + (3 \oplus 4) + (4 \oplus 3) = 15(2ÔŐĽ3)+(3ÔŐĽ4)+(4ÔŐĽ3)=15.
